zh.lifeSummary: 當Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara於1090年出生在西班牙加利西亞時，他的父親Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara35歲，母親Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón29歲。他和Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal至少有2個女兒。他去世於1155年11月1日在西班牙加利西亞武倫瑟A 培洛薩布巴爾享年65歲，埋葬在羅馬尼亞艾巴多斯。

nameMeanings.name: Pérez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Traba
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in Trøndelag, derived from trane ‘crane’. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 423, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

en.lifeSummary: When Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara was born in 1090, in Galicia, Spain, his father, Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara, was 35 and his mother, Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, was 29. He had at least 2 daughters with Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. He died on 1 November 1155, in Búbal, A Peroxa, Ourense, Galicia, Spain, at the age of 65, and was buried in Dos, Alba, Romania.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara est né en 1090, à Galicia, Espagne, son père, Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Il est décédé le 1 novembre 1155, à Búbal, A Peroxa, Ourense, Galicia, Espagne, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Dos, Alba, Roumanie.

ko.lifeSummary: Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara(이)가 1090년 스페인 갈리샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1155년 11월 1일 스페인 갈리샤 우렌스 아 페록사 브u바에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 루마니아 알바 도스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara nasceu em 1090, em Galicia, Espanha, seu pai, Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, tinha 29 anos. Ele faleceu em 1 de novembro de 1155, em Búbal, A Peroxa, Ourense, Galicia, Espanha, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Dos, Alba, Romênia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara родился в 1090 г. в местности галися, Испания, его отцу по имени Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, 29. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Он умер 1 ноября 1155 г. в местности бuбаль, а пирокса, оуренс, галися, Испания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности дос, алба, Румыния.
